# BoundingBox { #astroapers.BoundingBox }

```python
BoundingBox(ixmin, ixmax, iymin, iymax)
```

Pixel-index bounding box with exclusive upper bounds.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**ixmin**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Inclusive lower and exclusive upper x pixel indices.

<code>[**ixmax**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Inclusive lower and exclusive upper x pixel indices.

<code>[**iymin**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Inclusive lower and exclusive upper y pixel indices.

<code>[**iymax**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Inclusive lower and exclusive upper y pixel indices.

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

Bounding boxes may extend outside an image. Use
:meth:`overlap_slices` to obtain matching image and bbox-tight mask slices.

## Attributes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [shape](#astroapers.BoundingBox.shape) | Mask-array shape as ``(ny, nx)``. |

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [apsum](#astroapers.BoundingBox.apsum) | Return aperture sum, and npix by default, for raw aperture weights. |
| [npix](#astroapers.BoundingBox.npix) | Return the in-frame sum of raw aperture weights for ``shape``. |
| [overlap_slices](#astroapers.BoundingBox.overlap_slices) | Return image and mask slices for the overlap with ``shape``. |
| [to_fits_section](#astroapers.BoundingBox.to_fits_section) | Return this box as a FITS image section string. |
| [to_image](#astroapers.BoundingBox.to_image) | Embed bbox-tight mask weights in a full image array. |
| [weighted_cutout](#astroapers.BoundingBox.weighted_cutout) | Return a bbox-tight weighted cutout for raw aperture weights. |
| [weighted_values](#astroapers.BoundingBox.weighted_values) | Return weighted values for positive raw aperture weights. |

### apsum { #astroapers.BoundingBox.apsum }

```python
BoundingBox.apsum(weights, image, mask=None, *, return_npix=True, validate=True)
```

Return aperture sum, and npix by default, for raw aperture weights.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**weights**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Bbox-tight aperture weights with shape ``self.shape``.

<code>[**image**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Two-dimensional image to sample.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with the same shape as ``image``. ``True`` pixels contribute zero weight.

<code>[**return_npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `True`, return ``(apsum, npix)``. If `False`, return only ``apsum``.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `False`, trust that ``weights``, ``image``, and ``mask`` are already valid and aligned.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**apsum**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sum of ``image * weight`` over the overlapping, unmasked footprint. Accumulation uses `float64`, even when both the image and stored weights are `float32`.

<code>[**npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sum of the contributing aperture weights. This can be smaller than the analytic aperture area when the aperture is clipped by the image edge or by ``mask``. Accumulation uses `float64`. Returned only when ``return_npix=True``.

### npix { #astroapers.BoundingBox.npix }

```python
BoundingBox.npix(weights, shape, mask=None, *, validate=True)
```

Return the in-frame sum of raw aperture weights for ``shape``.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**weights**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Bbox-tight aperture weights with shape ``self.shape``.

<code>[**shape**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple\[int, int\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Full image shape as ``(ny, nx)``. This controls image-edge clipping.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with shape ``shape``. ``True`` pixels are excluded from the returned effective pixel count.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `False`, trust that ``weights``, ``shape``, and ``mask`` are already valid and aligned.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sum of in-frame, unmasked aperture weights. Accumulation uses `float64`, even when the stored weights are `float32`.

### overlap_slices { #astroapers.BoundingBox.overlap_slices }

```python
BoundingBox.overlap_slices(shape)
```

Return image and mask slices for the overlap with ``shape``.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**shape**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple\[int, int\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Full image shape as ``(ny, nx)``.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple of tuple of slice, or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``(data_slices, mask_slices)`` when the bounding box overlaps the image. ``data_slices`` select the in-frame pixels from the full image; ``mask_slices`` select the matching region from the bbox-tight mask. Returns ``None`` when the bounding box is fully outside the image.

### to_fits_section { #astroapers.BoundingBox.to_fits_section }

```python
BoundingBox.to_fits_section(shape=None)
```

Return this box as a FITS image section string.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**shape**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple\[int, int\], optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Full image shape as ``(ny, nx)``. If supplied, the returned section is clipped to the image overlap. If omitted, the raw bounding box must already be valid in FITS coordinates.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   FITS-standard image section in 1-indexed, ``x,y`` order, with inclusive upper bounds.

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

<code>[**ValueError**]{.parameter-name}</code>

:   If the raw bounding box has nonpositive extent, if an unclipped box would start before FITS pixel 1, or if the box does not overlap ``shape``.

### to_image { #astroapers.BoundingBox.to_image }

```python
BoundingBox.to_image(weights, shape, fill_value=0.0, *, validate=True)
```

Embed bbox-tight mask weights in a full image array.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**weights**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Bbox-tight aperture weights with shape ``self.shape``.

<code>[**shape**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple\[int, int\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Output image shape as ``(ny, nx)``.

<code>[**fill_value**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Value assigned to pixels outside the aperture mask footprint.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `False`, trust that ``weights`` and ``shape`` are already valid.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Floating image with shape ``shape``. If this bounding box is partly outside the image, only the overlapping weights are copied. If it is fully outside, the returned image contains only ``fill_value``.

### weighted_cutout { #astroapers.BoundingBox.weighted_cutout }

```python
BoundingBox.weighted_cutout(
    weights,
    image,
    mask=None,
    fill_value=np.nan,
    *,
    validate=True,
)
```

Return a bbox-tight weighted cutout for raw aperture weights.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**weights**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Bbox-tight aperture weights with shape ``self.shape``.

<code>[**image**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Two-dimensional image to sample.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with the same shape as ``image``. ``True`` pixels are excluded by setting their aperture weights to zero before multiplication.

<code>[**fill_value**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [np.nan]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Value used in returned cutout pixels that do not overlap ``image``.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `False`, trust that ``weights``, ``image``, and ``mask`` are already valid and aligned.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Floating cutout with shape ``self.shape``. Overlapping pixels contain ``image * weight``; non-overlapping pixels contain ``fill_value``.

### weighted_values { #astroapers.BoundingBox.weighted_values }

```python
BoundingBox.weighted_values(weights, image, mask=None, *, validate=True)
```

Return weighted values for positive raw aperture weights.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**weights**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Bbox-tight aperture weights with shape ``self.shape``.

<code>[**image**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Two-dimensional image to sample.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with the same shape as ``image``. ``True`` pixels are omitted from the returned vector.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `False`, trust that ``weights``, ``image``, and ``mask`` are already valid and aligned.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   One-dimensional floating array containing ``image * weight`` for in-frame pixels with positive aperture weight. Masked pixels and off-image pixels are omitted.
